title: Enforce Human Decision Gate and Resume Protocol
problemStatement: A structured Human Decision Request can be emitted, but there is no tamper-evident handoff that proves which decision was made for which Contract and Preflight state, nor a fail-closed recovery path.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Gate disabled with a ready Contract
scenarioCoverage.expected: Existing advisory behavior remains unchanged and implementation may proceed.
Preflight and lifecycle entry fail closed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Decision Evidence has stale or mismatched hashes
scenarioCoverage.expected: The evidence is rejected and the Work Item cannot resume.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Valid Decision Evidence followed by a fresh ready Preflight
scenarioCoverage.expected: The lifecycle may resume and finish after the current state is recomputed ready.

outOfScope: product source code outside the AI Cockpit workflow

sources.reason: Work Item 2 objective, file boundaries, and required state transitions.
Existing Preflight status, gate policy, report hash, and structured request implementation.
Work Item start path that invokes code-mode Preflight.
Work Item finish path that must enforce the gate before lifecycle closure.
Repository policy switch for advisory versus blocking Preflight states.
Existing Preflight and policy behavior regression coverage.

riskAssessment.reason: The change protects implementation and finish transitions when a repository enables the explicit Preflight Gate; the default advisory policy remains unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized the implementation plan and normal branch, verification, PR, merge, release, and cleanup workflow.

preReviewWarnings: Review evidence binding, stale-hash rejection, and fail-closed behavior when the Gate is enabled.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Decision Evidence records decisionId, decision, workItemId, contractHash, preflightHash, recordedAt, and recordedBy, and is accepted only when its identity and hashes match the current Work Item and current Preflight report.

A Gate-enabled Preflight rejects missing, stale, wrong-Contract, or not-yet-recomputed Decision Evidence; the report exposes human_decision_recorded only for a valid decision against the current non-ready Preflight.
ai-start and ai-finish fail closed when the Gate blocks, while the default gate-disabled advisory behavior remains backward compatible.
After Decision Evidence is recorded and Preflight is rerun, only a ready report permits the implementation and ready_for_review lifecycle paths; invalid or missing evidence never resumes work.

Regression tests cover no decision, stale Preflight Hash, wrong Contract Hash, no re-Preflight, valid ready recovery, and unchanged advisory behavior; documentation describes the evidence schema and operator flow.
guidelines: Keep Decision Evidence generic, repository-local, and agent agnostic.
Bind every accepted decision to the exact Work Item identity, Contract Hash, and current Preflight Hash.
Fail closed only when gateEnabled is true; preserve advisory behavior when the policy is disabled.
Do not treat a recorded decision as implementation authorization until a fresh Preflight report is ready.

intent.problem: A structured Human Decision Request can be emitted, but there is no tamper-evident handoff that proves which decision was made for which Contract and Preflight state, nor a fail-closed recovery path.
constraints: Decision Evidence must be bound to the current Work Item, Contract Hash, and Preflight Hash.
The default gate-disabled policy must remain advisory and backward compatible.
A decision alone cannot resume implementation; a fresh Preflight must recompute to ready.
Do not add product behavior or provider-specific approval semantics.
intent.rationale: Hash-bound evidence separates a human decision from the later verification that the repository is safe to resume, preventing stale or misapplied approvals from authorizing implementation.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Explicit user authorization covers this plan's normal governance-file, test, documentation, branch, PR, merge, and cleanup changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
